# Team News: Ecuador vs. Curacao injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 20 Jun 2026 01:00

© Iconsport / SUSA

Group E action at the 2026 World Cup continues in Kansas on Saturday night as Ecuador and debutants Curacao both look to bounce back from opening-match disappointments.

Sebastian Beccacece's side saw their remarkable 19-match unbeaten streak snapped by a late Ivory Coast winner, while Dick Advocaat's underdogs must find a way to sustain the resilience they showed during parts of their heavy 7-1 defeat to Germany.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest team news for both nations.

---

## ECUADOR vs. CURACAO

## ECUADOR

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Galindez; Franco, Pacho, Ordonez, Hincapie; Yeboah, Caicedo, Vite, Angulo; Valencia, Plata

## CURACAO

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Room; Floranus, Bazoer, Obispo, Fonville; Comenencia, L. Bacuna, J. Bacuna; Chong; Locadia, Hansen

---

Written by

Share this article:

Subscribe to ou

news_items.text:
Ecuador vs Curacao team news and predicted lineups | Squawka | EN

Squawka/ News/ World Cup/ Ecuador vs Curacao team news and predicted lineups

# Ecuador vs Curacao team news and predicted lineups

- Luke Hamilton
- 11 hours ago

- Copied

Ecuador and Curacao return to action at 2026 World Cup in the early hours of Sunday 21 June at 1am (UK time).

Ecuador sit third in the Group E after a devastating stoppage time loss to Ivory Coast. Sebastian Beccacece’s side lacked a clinical edge in the final third but still showed enough to suggest they should grow into the tournament. They will view this as a must-win opportunity to build momentum before the final group fixture with Germany.

Curacao, meanwhile, were comfortably beaten in their opener, but did manage to get themselves on the scoresheet. The debutants struggled to cope defensively against sustained pressure, and were overwhelmed by the sheer quality and volume of Germany. Another demanding evening looks likely against an Ecuador side eager to assert themselves.

The two sides have never met before, adding a fresh dynamic to this group-stage encounter. That being said, Ecuador will be overwhelming favourites given their superi

# Curacao lineup vs. Ecuador: Predicted XI for crucial World Cup encounter including Livano Comenencia decision

By Seye Omidiora| 19 Jun 2026 05:31

© Iconsport / ZUMA Press Wire

History maker Livano Comenencia will hope to retain his place in the starting lineup and find the back of the net once again when Curacao Ecuador on Saturday night.

The midfielder scored the Blue Wave's first-ever World Cup goal to level for the debutants in the eventual 7-1 defeat against Germany on Sunday, and he is expected to feature from the off as the experienced Dick Advocaat boasts a completely healthy squad to choose from following an unscathed opening fixture.

While that historic strike was only Comenencia's third for his country, Juninho Bacuna and captain Leandro Bacuna, who have registered 14 and 16 goals respectively, are expected to shoulder much of the attacking burden for the Tricolours.

Further forward, former Man Utd forward Tahith Chong is set to play an important role in the final third, alth

# Curacao's predicted lineup vs Ecuador for FIFA World Cup 2026

By Saikat Sengupta

Follow

Set As Preferred Source

Saikat has been the Khel Now Correspondent of Jamshedpur FC, alongside Indian football he writes world football & Hockey articles.

Published at :June 19, 2026 at 10:57 PM

Modified at :June 19, 2026 at 10:57 PM

## Can debutants Curacao shock Ecuador in a must-win game after a big defeat to Germany?

Anchored in the heart of the American Midwest, Kansas City Stadium (GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium) in Missouri, USA, gears up to host a highly anticipated Group E fixture of Matchweek 2 in the FIFA World Cup 2026, where South American giants Ecuador take on the Blue Wave in what is set to be a thrilling battle.

Curacao are coming after a humiliating 7-1 defeat to Germany in the opening matchweek. When the Blue Wave scored that equaliser against Germany, everybody thought that a big upset was underway.

But what followed next was de

# Team News: Ecuador vs. Curacao injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Seye Omidiora| 20 Jun 2026 01:00

© Iconsport / SUSA

Group E action at the 2026 World Cup continues in Kansas on Saturday night as Ecuador and debutants Curacao both look to bounce back from opening-match disappointments.

Sebastian Beccacece's side saw their remarkable 19-match unbeaten streak snapped by a late Ivory Coast winner, while Dick Advocaat's underdogs must find a way to sustain the resilience they showed during parts of their heavy 7-1 defeat to Germany.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest team news for both nations.

---

## ECUADOR vs. CURACAO

## ECUADOR

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Galindez; Franco, Pacho, Ordonez, Hincapie; Yeboah, Caicedo, Vite, Angulo; Valencia, Plata

## CURACAO

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Room; Floranus, Bazoer, Obispo, Fonville; Comenencia, L. Bacuna, J. Bacuna; Chong; Locadia, Hansen

---

Written by

# Ecuador vs Curacao team news and predicted lineups

- Luke Hamilton
- 11 hours ago

- Copied

Ecuador and Curacao return to action at 2026 World Cup in the early hours of Sunday 21 June at 1am (UK time).

Ecuador sit third in the Group E after a devastating stoppage time loss to Ivory Coast. Sebastian Beccacece’s side lacked a clinical edge in the final third but still showed enough to suggest they should grow into the tournament. They will view this as a must-win opportunity to build momentum before the final group fixture with Germany.

Curacao, meanwhile, were comfortably beaten in their opener, but did manage to get themselves on the scoresheet. The debutants struggled to cope defensively against sustained pressure, and were overwhelmed by the sheer quality and volume of Germany. Another demanding evening looks likely against an Ecuador side eager to assert themselves.

The two sides have never met before, adding a fresh dynamic to this group-stage encounter. That being said, Ecuador will be overwhelming favourites given their superi"}

# Curacao lineup vs. Ecuador: Predicted XI for crucial World Cup encounter including Livano Comenencia decision

By Seye Omidiora| 19 Jun 2026 05:31

© Iconsport / ZUMA Press Wire

History maker Livano Comenencia will hope to retain his place in the starting lineup and find the back of the net once again when Curacao Ecuador on Saturday night.

The midfielder scored the Blue Wave's first-ever World Cup goal to level for the debutants in the eventual 7-1 defeat against Germany on Sunday, and he is expected to feature from the off as the experienced Dick Advocaat boasts a completely healthy squad to choose from following an unscathed opening fixture.

While that historic strike was only Comenencia's third for his country, Juninho Bacuna and captain Leandro Bacuna, who have registered 14 and 16 goals respectively, are expected to shoulder much of the attacking burden for the Tricolours.

Further forward, former Man Utd forward Tahith Chong is set to play an important role in the final third, alth"}

# Curacao's predicted lineup vs Ecuador for FIFA World Cup 2026

By Saikat Sengupta

Follow

Set As Preferred Source

Saikat has been the Khel Now Correspondent of Jamshedpur FC, alongside Indian football he writes world football & Hockey articles.

Published at :June 19, 2026 at 10:57 PM

Modified at :June 19, 2026 at 10:57 PM

## Can debutants Curacao shock Ecuador in a must-win game after a big defeat to Germany?

Anchored in the heart of the American Midwest, Kansas City Stadium (GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium) in Missouri, USA, gears up to host a highly anticipated Group E fixture of Matchweek 2 in the FIFA World Cup 2026, where South American giants Ecuador take on the Blue Wave in what is set to be a thrilling battle.

Curacao are coming after a humiliating 7-1 defeat to Germany in the opening matchweek. When the Blue Wave scored that equaliser against Germany, everybody thought that a big upset was underway.
